title: [v4.0-rhel] Bump Buildah to v1.24.7
body:
This bumps Buildah to v1.24.7 and addresses CVE-2024-1753 
https://issues.redhat.com/browse/RHEL-26758
https://issues.redhat.com/browse/RHEL-26757
https://issues.redhat.com/browse/RHEL-26756

[NO NEW TESTS NEEDED]

<!--
Thanks for sending a pull request!

Please make sure you've read our contributing guidelines and how to submit a pull request (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md#submitting-pull-requests).

In case you're only changing docs, make sure to prefix the pull-request title with "[CI:DOCS]". That will prevent functional tests from running and save time and energy.

Finally, be sure to sign commits with your real name. Since by opening
a PR you already have commits, you can add signatures if needed with
something like `git commit -s --amend`.
-->

#### Does this PR introduce a user-facing change?

<!--
If no, just write `None` in the release-note block below. If yes, a release note
is required: Enter your extended release note in the block below. If the PR
requires additional action from users switching to the new release, include the
string "action required".

For more information on release notes, please follow the Kubernetes model:
https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/release-notes.md
-->

```release-note
None
```

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *TomSweeneyRedHat* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/v4.0-rhel/OWNERS)~~ [TomSweeneyRedHat] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

@cevich the go issue that's popping up in the F35 test failure here seems to be because it's not using Go 1.17.  I made what I thought was the appropriate changes to go.mod and the Makefile.  Did I miss another spot?

> Did I miss another spot?

Yeah, go 1.16 is installed in the VMs :cry:

There's no automatic way to update the VMs permanently, and doing so could affect other repo's CI.  I'd suggest a runtime upgrade but...Nope, `1.16.15-3.fc35` is the latest available in the F35 repos.

The only option I can think of is perhaps a runtime binary install from https://go.dev/dl/ but my gosh that seems fragile.  Very special care would be needed to override `$GOBIN` and `$GOTOOLDIR` from all the different contexts :confused: 

Maybe rip and replace the CI setup to use F36 is an option?  Otherwise if this problem isn't fixed, once this PR merges CI will forever be broken on this branch.

Thoughts?
I think the best option is to kill F35 and move to F36 for this branch.  @mheon any concerns with that?  If Matt is OK with that, @cevich can you make that happen please, and then I'll rebase this?

> @cevich can you make that happen please, and then I'll rebase this?

I'll certainly give it a try, hopefully it will "just work"...
@TomSweeneyRedHat this is ready to be rebased.  Hopefully the changes I made will help.

@cevich I'm still getting a F35 error?  Is that GitHub hanging onto the last failure?  I thought you had removed that particular test.  I'm wondering if I should just kill this PR and start from scratch.

> I'm still getting a F35 error? Is that GitHub hanging onto the last failure? I thought you had removed that particular test.

You rebased correctly, this simply means changes in this PR are not happy with F35 and golang 1.16.

> I'm wondering if I should just kill this PR and start from scratch.

IMHO no, I think this is the CI system letting you down, not the other way around.  Let me see...

```
vendor/github.com/fsnotify/fsnotify/inotify.go:20:2: //go:build comment without // +build comment
```

Oof, I very vaguely remember encountering this before.  Unf. I don't remember the fix.  I think it was to add the `// +build` line, but it was in our own code, not vendor code :disappointed:  Though this was one of the modules you touched.  Hmmm :thinking:

Here's an idea (don't know how helpful it will be).  Use the similar-to container image to work locally on fixing this:

`quay.io/libpod/fedora_podman:c5814666029957120`

You can volume mount your code into that container and should be able to reproduce the problem.  Assuming so, the first thing I would try is to see if a newer version or older version of `vendor/github.com/fsnotify` allows the build to progress.  I fear the result may be, you simply run into the same/similar error with another package, but maybe there's a chance?

It may be worth while bringing in some of our more expert golang folks, like Miloslav or Paul or Matt.  They'd likely do better than my guesses.  In any case, I think trying to fix it locally using that container might allow you to iterate quicker.  Esp. w/ all the github outages we've had today.

Hope that helps.  Ping me again if not.
I think you're dragging in packages that are too new for Go 1.16, the new `go:build` comments are 1.17 and above.
@cevich I'll hit you up in Slack tomorrow.
Do we know for sure that RHEL we're shipping this on has Go 1.17?
@cevich I've repushed.  @mheon, I'd be surprised if RHEL 8.6/9.0 did not have at least Go 1.17, but I'll see if I can verify with Jindrich.
